note: Per-run records. worldgen = macro-averaged per-block-class accuracy vs the real Java game over time-seeds drawn after the run froze (v0 scores the worldgen leg; sim tick-trace and fidelity-gated SPS land next). Raw artifacts: benchmarks/rewrite/results/runs/.

runs.note: INFRA, not a fail: killed mid-refactor by Max-plan credit exhaustion (429 wall at 61 min, 112 turns, 1.24M output tokens), leaving the tree uncompilable. Also the only run attempting vanilla-exact worldgen (Java Random, noise, carving) and the only one using git. Rerun pending
leader; first to crack sea level - water 88%, lava 55%, air 99.5% - and first whose raw match ties all-stone while macro is +11 over it; ores/trees/caves still ~0%
current leader; only model with real water placement (50%) and it grinds longest so far - but ores, trees, vegetation, caves still ~0%
best stone shaping of the sweep (78.8%) but nothing beyond air/stone/bedrock; initial eval-container header mismatch was a harness bug, rescored after parity fix
builds, but violates the dump contract twice: 32-byte .mcbd header (drops dim/reserved fields) and --out path handling writes to a corrupted filename - no scoreable dump
cheapest scored run; air/stone/bedrock only, weakest air placement of the sweep (83.5%) - everything else ~0%
self-terminated at 15 min of 8h; ABI/CLI/CUDA stepping built clean in the air-gapped container, but worldgen is an admitted approximation - ores, trees, water, caves near 0%

baselines.name: all-stone
baselines.note: trivial floor: bedrock + stone to y63 + air; beats gpt-5.5 on raw match
